name: ACI.dev (Agent Computing Infrastructure)
what_it_does: ACI.dev (Agent Computing Infrastructure) is an open-source platform providing a unified API for 600+ tools that AI agents can use. Acts as a managed tool registry and execution layer — agents discover and call tools via ACI without managing each tool's auth, rate limits, and schemas individually.
use_cases: Building agents that need access to many different tools without managing each API individually
Unified authentication layer for multi-tool agent workflows
Tool discovery: agents query ACI to find tools for specific tasks
Reducing agent context window usage by handling tool schema management
Standardized tool calling interface across 600+ services
not_for: Teams needing SOC2/enterprise compliance today (early-stage startup)
Use cases requiring custom tool schemas or complex auth flows
best_when: Your agent needs to use many different tools and you want a unified, managed interface instead of integrating each API separately.
avoid_when: You only need 1-2 tools, or need enterprise-grade compliance and SLAs.

auth.notes: ACI API key for platform access. OAuth flows for connecting individual tools (Slack, GitHub, etc.) are managed by ACI — agents don't handle per-tool auth directly.

pricing.free_tier_limits: Generous free tier for development; specific limits not publicly documented
pricing.paid_tiers: Usage-based pricing for production; contact for enterprise
pricing.notes: Open-source self-hostable version available. Managed cloud offering with free tier for development.

performance.rate_limits: Per-tool rate limits apply via ACI; ACI itself adds minimal overhead

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: Error messages clearly indicate whether the error is in ACI routing or in the underlying tool. Tool-level errors are passed through with context.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: Depends on the underlying tool's idempotency. ACI itself doesn't add idempotency.
known_agent_gotchas: Early-stage product — API changes more frequently than established tools
600+ tools means quality varies significantly by tool — some integrations are deeper than others
Self-hosted version requires significant DevOps setup
Tool auth tokens stored by ACI — trust in ACI's security model is required
Not all tools in the registry are equally well-tested for agent use cases
Response latency varies widely by underlying tool
agent_readiness.security_notes: ACI holds OAuth tokens for connected tools — significant trust assumption. Audit logs available. Open-source code is auditable. Early-stage security posture.
